产气机理__产气机理2__产气机理3.gold_answer: Choose internal temperature (or, if not feasible, a proxy for self-heating like pressure rate-of-rise tied to rapid gas generation) because runaway is defined by internally accelerating heat generation: overheating triggers TR and decomposition reactions produce more heat/gas. The discrimination logic must hinge on detecting internal self-acceleration (dT/dt increasing without added external heating), not merely presence of gas.

Support with conditions: Evidence supports that adding an immiscible, separator‑wetting additive can localize at/within the separator and modify interfacial wetting/transport in a way that can reduce the severity of abuse reactions, including lowering or delaying peak heat generation/temperature rise during abusive events, while still allowing acceptable ion transport under normal cycling if concentration/viscosity are controlled.

Not supported: The evidence does not demonstrate that such an additive prevents pack‑level fires, guarantees elimination of internal shorts, or ensures no thermal runaway propagation. It also does not prove protection under all abuse modes (crush, nail, external heating, overcharge) or across full‑size cells/packs, where venting, gas generation, mechanical damage, and module thermal coupling dominate outcomes. Therefore the additive can be justified as a cell‑level abuse‑severity mitigant, not as a definitive fire‑prevention or short‑circuit‑proofing solution.
工程防护策略1-001__工程防护策略1-002__工程防护策略1-003.gold_answer: Supported: the additive can form a blocking layer in the separator that suppresses Li+ transport, and tests show large reductions in peak temperature during penetration/impact while 1C cycling behavior can remain nearly unchanged. 建模预测71__建模预测94__建模预测95.prediction:
Rule-set using only cell surface temperature T and rise-rate dT/dt:
1) Normal: T < 60°C AND dT/dt < 0.02°C/s.
2) Self-heating onset (stage i): declare when (T ≥ 60°C AND dT/dt ≥ 0.02°C/s) persists for ≥30 s, OR when dT/dt increases by ≥0.02°C/s above its 5-min baseline while T ≥ 40°C for ≥30 s.
3) TR trigger (stage ii): declare when any of the following persists for ≥10 s: (a) T ≥ 120°C, OR (b) dT/dt ≥ 0.2°C/s, OR (c) T ≥ 90°C AND dT/dt ≥ 0.1°C/s.
4) Post-trigger propagation risk to neighbor (stage iii): declare “high propagation risk” when stage (ii) is true AND (T ≥ 150°C OR dT/dt ≥ 0.5°C/s) for ≥10 s; declare “critical propagation risk” when (T ≥ 200°C OR dT/dt ≥ 1.0°C/s) for ≥5 s. If risk is high/critical, command mitigation (isolation/cooling/shutdown) assuming adjacent cell at risk.

Limitations (non-unique diagnosis with only T and dT/dt):
A) The controller cannot uniquely distinguish internal exothermic self-heating from external heating (heater contact, ambient/hot gas exposure) that produces similar T and dT/dt.
B) The controller cannot uniquely identify the internal failure mode (SEI breakdown vs separator damage vs vent/combustion) or SOC/state-of-health effects; the same thresholds can occur for different mechanisms.
C) Surface T and dT/dt may lag core temperature; a cell can be in internal runaway while surface signals are delayed, so “stage” classification may be late or ambiguous.
D) Propagation risk to a neighbor cannot be uniquely inferred without geometry, spacing, and thermal contact; the same cell T/dT/dt can correspond to very different heat flux to adjacent cells.
建模预测71__建模预测94__建模预测95.gold_answer: Defensible rules: (i) declare self-heating onset when measured temperature rise-rate exceeds a chosen empirical threshold (T1 criterion). (ii) declare TR trigger only when temperature transitions from a stable/slowly varying level into a quasi-exponential, self-sustaining rise (T2 behavior), not merely from T1. (iii) for propagation risk, treat it as increasing when thermal coupling is stronger (lower equivalent thermal resistance via smaller spacing) and/or ambient temperature is higher; near a coupling-resistance threshold, small changes can shift propagation mode. Limitations: surface measurements may not equal jellyroll temperature, so ISC at separator melting cannot be uniquely inferred; crossing reaction temperature thresholds does not by itself prove TR trigger; timing between cells does not uniquely identify coupling resistance or mechanism.
建模预测71__建模预测94__建模预测95.reason: Meets (i) by defining self-heating onset via a dT/dt threshold and includes multiple valid non-identifiability limitations (surface vs core lag, external vs internal heating, mechanism ambiguity, geometry unknown). However, (ii) is not aligned with the gold: it declares TR trigger mainly by absolute T or dT/dt thresholds rather than explicitly requiring a transition into a quasi-exponential/self-sustaining rise, and it risks the disallowed idea that crossing temperature thresholds alone indicates trigger. For (iii), it notes spacing/thermal contact affects propagation risk, but does not incorporate ambient temperature dependence into the rule-set itself.
